news.headline: Hong Kong car owner loses HK$500,000 in fuel membership card scam
news.summary:
A Hong Kong car owner has lost HK$500,000 (US$63,800) to scammers posing as petrol company staff who exploited surging fuel prices by offering bogus top-up deals for membership cards, police have said.

news.headline: ‘We’re new miyas’: Will BJP naming some Assamese Muslims ‘Indigenous’ work?
news.summary: As BJP reaches out to 4 million Muslims in the poll-bound northeastern state, many remain sceptical of party's agenda.

news.headline: US-Iran ceasefire: 'Still unclear what is to be negotiated'
news.summary: Donald Trump has added more confusion to the terms of the ceasefire agreed with Iran by telling AFP that the US had a “15-point” proposal for talks set to take place in the next two weeks, having earlier said that Iran had presented a 10-point plan, reports France 24’s correspondent in Washington Fr

news.headline: Declarations of victory in Iran after ceasefire agreed with US
news.summary: An agreement for a two-week ceasefire with the US and Israel is being hailed as a victory in Iran, reports France 24's correspondent Reza Sayah, with state media claiming the deal is "yet another retreat" by US president Donald Trump.

news.headline: Gulf countries report attacks despite Iran ceasefire
news.summary: A number of Gulf states have reported being targeted by Iranian strikes despite the ceasefire agreed between Iran, the US and Israel. news.headline: Hungary's Orban plays on energy price fears in re-election bid
news.summary: Much of Hungary's electoral campaign has centred around energy policy, at least in incumbent Viktor Orban's camp, as he seeks re-election amid the world's worst energy crisis in decades.
